mission.title: Install and Configure External Secrets on Kubernetes
mission.description: The External Secrets Operator reads information from third-party secret management services and automatically injects the values as Kubernetes Secrets. This allows for secure management of sensitive data in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the External Secrets Helm repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add external-secrets https://charts.external-secrets.io
```

steps.title: Update Helm repositories
steps.description:
Update your local Helm repository cache to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the External Secrets Operator
steps.description:
Install the External Secrets Operator using Helm. This command will create the namespace if it does not exist:
```bash
helm install external-secrets external-secrets/external-secrets --namespace external-secrets --create-namespace --version 2.0.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the External Secrets Operator pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace external-secrets
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the External Secrets Operator by editing the Helm release:
```bash
helm upgrade external-secrets external-secrets/external-secrets --namespace external-secrets --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=128Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the External Secrets Operator Helm release:
```bash
helm uninstall external-secrets --namespace external-secrets
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by the Helm chart:
```bash
kubectl delete crd externalsecrets.external-secrets.io
kubectl delete crd clusterexternalsecrets.external-secrets.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for the External Secrets Operator:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace external-secrets
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the External Secrets Operator:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace external-secrets -o yaml > external-secrets-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the External Secrets Operator
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the External Secrets Operator to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade external-secrets external-secrets/external-secrets --namespace external-secrets --version 2.0.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the pods are running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace external-secrets
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the External Secrets Operator pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace external-secrets
```
Common issues may include misconfigured secrets or permissions. Ensure that the external secret management service is accessible and properly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Failed to create Kubernetes Secret
troubleshooting.description:
If the operator fails to create a Kubernetes Secret, check the status of the ExternalSecret resource:
```bash
kubectl describe externalsecret <name> --namespace external-secrets
```
Look for events indicating why the secret creation failed, such as missing permissions or incorrect configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails, check for any pending changes or issues with the release:
```bash
helm status external-secrets --namespace external-secrets
```
You may need to roll back to a previous version if the upgrade cannot be resolved.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of the External Secrets Operator will result in the operator running in the 'external-secrets' namespace with all pods in a 'Running' state. You can verify this by checking the pod status using 'kubectl get pods --namespace external-secrets'.
codeSnippets: helm upgrade external-secrets external-secrets/external-secrets --namespace external-secrets --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=128Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
